name: Promote The Existing 128x128x32 Staged Hot-Band Kernel

summary.name: Promote The Existing 128x128x32 Staged Hot-Band Kernel
summary.hypothesis: The current default hot-band kernel is paying too much synchronization and wait overhead per 16-wide K tile. The existing 128x128x32 staged sibling already consumes two K tiles per stage and should reduce barrier and long-scoreboard pressure in the dominant hot-band region without changing the overall 128x128 CTA footprint.
summary.expected_bottleneck: synchronization_barrier_issue and long_scoreboard latency in the current hot-band 128x128 PTX microkernel
summary.risk: Low-to-moderate risk: the kernel already exists, but the alternate staging schedule may trade barrier relief for higher shared-memory traffic or a small register increase.
ranking_notes: This is the cheapest measured-stall hypothesis to test because the alternative kernel body is already in-tree.
It keeps the same hot-band CTA shape while directly changing the per-stage wait and barrier cadence.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if runtime does not improve by at least 0.3 ms or if barrier and long-scoreboard stalls stay flat within measurement noise.
expected_local_changes: Swap the default fixed-shape hot-band launch from the PTX microkernel path to the existing 128x128x32 staged kernel.
Keep the residual 64x384 row-band and 64x96 tail unchanged for the first measurement.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
